subject: science

primary.justification: The chapter's entire domain is the investigation of chemical substances — their classification into solutions, suspensions, and colloids; the quantitative expression of concentration (% m/m, % m/v, % v/v); solubility and its temperature dependence; and the selection and application of separation techniques including crystallization, distillation, paper chromatography, separating funnel (immiscible liquids), sublimation, centrifugation, and coagulation. Every topic is a facet of C-1.2's explicit mandate — 'types and properties of mixtures, solutions, colloids, and suspensions' and the separation techniques named therein. No distinct strand belonging to C-1.1 (atomic structure/valency) or C-1.3 (chemical equations) is present.

dissolution_test: This chapter builds the student's ability to investigate the nature, properties, and behaviour of chemical mixtures by classifying them into solutions, suspensions, and colloids; expressing concentration quantitatively using % m/m, % m/v, and % v/v; and selecting and applying appropriate separation techniques — including crystallization, distillation, paper chromatography, sublimation, centrifugation, and coagulation — based on the physical and chemical properties of the components.
